greeting: The canal hand cups both hands around a mug as if warming them over a stove. Mud dries in stripes on his trousers.
shrug: "The canal has words for that, maybe. I do not."

entries.text: "Call me a canal hand. If you know the locks, you know the man."
"Lines, locks, mule teams, freight. Honest work until night, then men ask no questions and pay in advance."
"This town thinks the road made it. The basin knows better. Everything heavy comes to water first."
"A creek can hide a thing for half a day. It cannot keep boots dry for a drowned man."
